programmes.show_description: Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
In Whom We Tryst: Comedy series about rival antique dealers. Simon and Oliver look in danger of losing more than their hearts when they both take a fancy to a comely shop assistant.
Vacancy for Inspector: Enduring British sitcom. Jack gets promoted to the role of inspector as Stan gets more than he bargained for when he invites a new conductor home for tea.
Marlon and Val argue when the chef learns that they didn't win the Pub of the Year competition. Sam offers to rebuild the factory, and Mel asks Ashley for time with Arthur.
Mel gives Greg an ultimatum when he confronts her about Arthur. Marlon prepares to contact the rival pub about the job offer but Donna urges him to reconsider.
Leanne tries to reason with Paul about his threat, but Dan decides to call his bluff. A remorseful David receives a visit from Audrey, and Kirk introduces his girlfriend to Fiz.
Leanne and Dan realise they're up to their necks in trouble as Paul heads off to the police station. Liam spends the night on Carla's sofa and Audrey finally arranges to meet Ted.
Conclusions: Feature-length drama. Inspector Frost has plenty on his plate when an MP's son is involved in a hit-and-run accident and a casino robbery takes place on the same night.
Over the Hill: Drama series about a policeman in Yorkshire in the 1960s. Nick investigates a series of break-ins that point to someone living rough in the woods.
Bang to Rights: Drama series about a policeman in Yorkshire in the 1960s. A chance happening prompts Nick to reopen an old case.
Second Sight: When Barnaby and Scott investigate the death of a man recently ejected from a pub for fighting, the post-mortem shows that he may have been subject to electric shocks.
Doug is fearful that Ashley's considering giving Arthur back to the Dolands. Bob and Viv make a decision on guardianship of the twins if they're both sent to jail.
A hung-over Liam makes his way home to Maria, but her fury turns to anguish when she receives devastating news at the hospital. Meanwhile, the police question Dan and Leanne.
Maria struggles to tell Liam the news as she tries to come to terms with what's happened. Roy is flummoxed by Becky's change of heart over Jason, and a nervous Audrey meets Ted.
A Talent for Deception: There are problems when PC Bellamy has to organise a talent contest, and Nick and Kate's relationship is put to the test when one of her old flames arrives.
Baby Blues: Drama series. Nick investigates the case of a baby stolen from its pram outside a shop where the prime suspects are a group of hippies who were busking nearby.
The Escape Turn: Crime drama series. Vera investigates when a wealthy betting shop magnate is shot and killed in front of his wife and daughter during a terrifying home invasion.
Left for Dead: Jones is brought back to his hometown to help investigate a series of murders linked to childhood friends and events that occurred years earlier.
Near Death Experience: Frost begins a manhunt for a serial killer when he uncovers a web of lies surrounding the investigation into the vicious murder of a widow.
The New Telly: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a bus company. Stan and his family decide to buy a colour TV, but there is a problem with the disposal of their old set.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Going Going Gone: Comedy series about rival antique dealers. Simon and Oliver find themselves at loggerheads over a relaxing weekend break.
A Thin Time: Sitcom about a two-man bus crew. Arthur is attracted to Beryl the clippie, but Beryl has only two interests in life - athletic men and those with a good head of hair.
Doug is fearful that Ashley's considering giving Arthur back to the Dolands. Bob and Viv make a decision on guardianship of the twins if they're both sent to jail.
A hung-over Liam makes his way home to Maria, but her fury turns to anguish when she receives devastating news at the hospital. Meanwhile, the police question Dan and Leanne.
Maria struggles to tell Liam the news as she tries to come to terms with what's happened. Roy is flummoxed by Becky's change of heart over Jason, and a nervous Audrey meets Ted.
A Minority of One: Feature-length drama. Frost finds his patience pushed to the limit when faced with the death of a police informer on an estate that is riven by racial tension.
A Talent for Deception: There are problems when PC Bellamy has to organise a talent contest, and Nick and Kate's relationship is put to the test when one of her old flames arrives.
Baby Blues: Drama series. Nick investigates the case of a baby stolen from its pram outside a shop where the prime suspects are a group of hippies who were busking nearby.
Hidden Depths: Barnaby and Scott investigate when a solicitor falls from a roof to his death. A revenge motive emerges when the dead man's rival also dies - but who is behind it?
Viv and Bob are stunned by Louise's suggestion she buy their businesses. Gennie worries she'll never be a proper Dingle, and Laurel makes progress with Arthur.
Debbie gets jealous when Eli opts to spend time with Gennie rather than her. Viv tells Louise she'll sell her the business, and Katie informs Gray and Perdy she wants a Caesarean.
Liam leaves for work as a distraught Maria packs her bag for the hospital, unable to find a way of telling him about the baby. Meanwhile, Jack learns Paul has been charged.
Maria continues to keep Liam in the dark as she heads back to the Street. Bill lets slip to a perplexed Gail about Audrey meeting Ted, and Liz refuses to go on Vernon's cruise.
Wall of Silence: Nick investigates an apparent crime wave in Aidensfield after a mysterious spate of burglaries, and the Assistant Chief Constable is due to visit Lord Ashfordly's.
Missing: Drama series set in the 1960s. The annual funfair comes to Aidensfield and causes much excitement - but some ill feeling too - before a five-year-old boy goes missing.
Witness: Crime drama series. Vera must unravel the mystery of a murdered family man found beaten at the Collingwood Monument the day before he was due to testify in court.
The Night of the Stag: Barnaby and Jones must hunt for a killer in a Midsomer village steeped in bizarre local traditions when a missing man turns up dead in a vat of cider.
The Gift of Promise: When a businesswoman is bludgeoned to death, it looks like a case of blackmail gone wrong but there is a decades-old secret at the heart of the case.
